  "findings": [
    "Severe belt mistracking caused by off-center material loading",
    "Potential hazard of belt edge damage and material spillage",
    "Risk of contact with structural frame violating OSHA 1910.212 guarding principles"

  "actions": [
    "Adjust the chute or loading point to center the material feed on the belt",
    "Inspect belt edges and structural clearances for wear or damage",
    "Verify idler alignment and tracking sensors are functional"
  ],
  "rationale": "The belt mistracking is primarily driven by off-center loading rather than idler skew, as evidenced by the tracking shifting dynamically with load changes. This creates a severe risk of belt damage, structural contact, and spillage, which violates safe operating conditions under CEMA guidelines and OSHA 1910.212 guarding standards.",

    "OSHA 1910.212"
  ]
